faqs.question: I am a Loughborough University Student/Staff member and can not activate my account
faqs.answer: If you can not activate your account after following our online joining process (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/join/) , this may be due to one of the following reasons; Your personal details are incorrect or your email address is not registered on our system, this will happen if; Please contact the sport memberships team and provide the following information for us to locate your account and assist; • We have not received confirmation of your enrolment from the University • You have previously changed your email address which differs to the email address you are trying to re-set your password with • You did a different course at the University previously, as your original student email address will remain on our system • You are a Loughborough College Student • Full Name • Date of Birth • Mobile Number • Student/Staff ID • University/College Email address

faqs.question: I am a community member, how do I sign up for an account?
faqs.answer: All new members will need to sign up for an account. To use our facilities on a casual basis please join as a Pay as You Go member for free. If you believe you have a registered account but can not activate this, please contact sportmemberships@lboro.ac.uk (mailto:sportmemberships@lboro.ac.uk) and provide the following information for us to locate your account and provide assistance; If you fall under our affiliate category (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/discount-categories/) , please ensure that you also send us proof of eligibility to reactivate your discount price. • Full name • Date of Birth • Mobile number

faqs.question: I can not use the app to gain access through the barriers
faqs.answer: To gain access through the barriers using your phone on our Check In tiles at Holywell, Powerbase, the Swimming Pool and HiPAC, please ensure that you have done the following; • Booked a session in the facility, the gates will not open unless a valid booking is present. • Logged into the Lboro Sport app with your credentials. • Update the Lboro Sport app to the latest version in the App Store and Google Play.

faqs.question: I am being charged for a session that is included in my membership
faqs.answer: If the app is charging you have an activity, please check the following; *Recent app updates now allow for members to book guests into classes. You will now see the guest price displayed on the booking page, however, if you are booking for yourself and hold a membership which includes classes this price will change to £0 once you add it to the basket and check out. • Your membership (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/) includes access to the activity you are trying to book at no additional charge • You are logged into the Lboro Sport app with your credentials. • Update the Lboro Sport app to the latest version in the App Store and Google Play.

faqs.question: I can not reset my password I do not receive the password reset email
faqs.answer: If you do not receive an email within 5 minutes of submitting the password request form, the following may apply; Your email address is not registered on our system, this will happen if; • We have not received confirmation of your enrolment from the University • You have previously changed your email address to the email address you are trying to re-set your password with • You did a different course at the University previously, as your original student email address will remain on our system

faqs.question: I am trying to purchase a membership but I keep getting the error 'This Membership is already held and so can not purchase again'
faqs.answer: This message will display when an attempt to purchase has been made but payment was not taken. To pay; • Go to the home screen of the Lboro Sports app • Select the tile titled My Account • Select the tile titled Bills & Payments • Log in if required • The unpaid membership subscription will display in the Bills section • Select the outstanding membership and follow the on screen instructions to proceed to payment

faqs.question: How do I join Powerbase?
faqs.answer: You can join Powerbase by purchasing the Gold membership option online or via the Loughborough Sport App. Those who can access a Gold membership are: Unfortunately if you do not qualify through one of these groups, it is not possible to join Powerbase as a Gold member, but you can attend on a pay as you go basis - pay as you go prices (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/pay-as-you-go-prices/) . You can also join Holywell Fitness Centre by purchasing a Silver membership which is open to everyone in the community. • current Loughborough University students • current Loughborough College students • alumni of Loughborough University • current Loughborough University staff • Key affiliates (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/discount-categories/) to Loughborough Sport

faqs.question: Do I have to pay for fitness classes with a gold membership?
faqs.answer: Complete access to our wide ranging fitness class programme is included with your gold membership. Members can sign up to classes in our studios at both the Sir David Wallace sports hall and Holywell Fitness Centre . You will need to book classes in advance to secure your place on all sessions.

faqs.question: What are elite training times?
faqs.answer: Powerbase is a recognised Elite Training Centre, home to many of the country's leading athletes including current and future Olympians and Paralympians. This means you get access to the same world-class gym used by these athletes, however, to facilitate their training, other members cannot access the same side(alternating between Power and Base) from 9:00-12:00 and 14:00-15:30 Monday to Friday and will have the option to only book on the other side (either Power or Base) or alternatively can use Holywell Fitness Centre during these times.

faqs.question: Is Powerbase two separate gyms and can I use both ?
faqs.answer: A multi-million pound extension opened in 2023 creates a new combined space of 3,200m 2 , making it one of the world's largest elite strength and conditioning environments. There are now two sides, POWER and BASE. You must book into one side or the other and train within the side you have booked. When entering Powerbase you will find POWER through the left turnstiles and BASE through the far right turnstiles.

faqs.question: How do I join Holywell?
faqs.answer: Holywell Fitness Centre is open to everyone! You can join Holywell by purchasing the Silver membership option online or via the Loughborough Sport App. If you are yet to download the app, please do, as all activities, including gym and pool sessions are bookable through the app. You can also attend Holywell on a pay as you go basis.

faqs.question: Can I access Powerbase gym as a Holywell member?
faqs.answer: Your Silver membership does not give you access to Powerbase, you would need to upgrade to a Gold membership, however please note that you must be eligible (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/) to obtain a Gold membership.

faqs.question: Do I have to pay for fitness classes as a member?
faqs.answer: Complete access to our wide ranging fitness class programme is included with your Holywell membership. Members can sign up to classes in our studios at both the Holywell Fitness Centre and Sir David Wallace sports hall . You will need to book classes in advance to secure your place on all sessions.

faqs.question: Do I have to pay for gym floor classes as a member?
faqs.answer: Access to our gym floor class programme is included with your Holywell membership. You will need to book classes in advance to secure your place on all sessions.

faqs.question: Do i have to book two 90 minute sessions back to back if i want to extend my workout?
faqs.answer: No. Remember you have a 15 minute grace period after the end of your 90 minute session to give you time to exit the gym. However, of additional time is required you can now book an overlapping session to extend your session from between 15 to 75 minutes.

faqs.question: If I've booked onto a coach-led performance session in Powerbase, does this policy apply?
faqs.answer: Yes. As with all gym sessions, they will last 90 minutes after which you have the same 15 minute grace period to exit the gym. All coaches are aware of this and should not be delaying your exit.

faqs.question: Do all classes have the same grace period as the gyms?
faqs.answer: No. As classes cannot be extended in the same way as gym sessions, both gym and floor classes in Powerbase and Holywell and studio classes in Holywell, have a 30 minute grace period after the class is schedule to end.

faqs.question: How many overstay vouchers do i get?
faqs.answer: Currently, all active members receive one voucher a month. They're valid for a month, then upon it's expiry, a new voucher will be added to your account as long as you have an active membership. If you've used your voucher, you will receive a new one at this point.

faqs.question: What can I access with my Bronze Vouchers
faqs.answer: To use the vouchers, make a booking through the Lboro Sport app and select use voucher to pay. Vouchers will expire after 3 months from allocation. Please note that the 1x Powerbase access has now replaced the 1x Class access that was previously included in the Bronze membership for 2023/24. These vouchers will remain valid after August if not used until the 3-month expiry date has passed. • 1x Powerbase access per 3 months (New for 2024/2025) • 1x Holywell access per 3 months

faqs.question: What can I access with my Silver Vouchers?
faqs.answer: To use the vouchers, make a booking through the Lboro Sport app and select use voucher to pay. Vouchers will expire after 3 months from allocation. • 4x Powerbase access per year for annual or academic year memberships • 1x Powerbase access per 3 months for monthly paying members

faqs.question: How do I request a refund?
faqs.answer: Refunds for memberships cannot be made within facilities. All refund requests need to be placed in writing by the membership holder. This can be done by completing the online cancellation request form (https://www.lboro.ac.uk/sport/memberships-prices/cancellations/) or emailing sportmemberships@lboro.ac.uk (mailto:sportmemberships@lboro.ac.uk) . Please allow up to 7 working days to receive a response, to allow us time to review your request and assess eligibility. Note - Reimbursements will not be refunded to members who are eligible for a discount as an Affiliate to Loughborough Sport after purchasing a membership prior to having their account status updated from community.

faqs.question: I have bought an academic year or annual membership, can I get a refund?
faqs.answer: No. If you can no longer use the gym, refunds will only be granted for medical conditions supported by a doctor’s letter or a leave of absence form from the Universit y.

faqs.question: Who's eligible for a refund?
faqs.answer: To be eligible for a refund for a membership paid upfront, the below criteria must be met: Those that are eligible to receive a refund, will be informed of the amount they are entitled to receive, after administration and usage fees are deducted. • The membership was purchased within 14 days of the cancellation request being submitted. • You are withdrawing from your studies or employment at Loughborough University - a signed and confirmed leave of absence letter from the University will be required to support the refund request. • You are currently unable to exercise due to medical reasons - a signed doctor's letter will be required to support the refund request, stating your name and a full explanation of why you are not able to train; we cannot except letters that are dated prior to the date your membership was purchased.

faqs.question: What are administration and usage fees?
faqs.answer: If the cancellation request has been made within 14 days of the date of purchase and no service has been used in relation to that membership, no additional fees will be applied to the refund. Joining fees and enrollment fees are non-refundable. If a service has been used, members will be charged on a pay as you go basis for each gym, track, pool and / or class use. This will be capped at the monthly fee for that membership (e.g a Silver gym membership of £32 per month or Gold gym membership of £40 per month). This total charge will be subtracted from the price paid. Additionally, if a joining or enrollment fee was paid during membership purchase, this is non-refundable. All refunds granted 14 days after the date of purchase, will incur a £20 administration fee plus a usage fee. For annual / academic year memberships deductions will be calculated through applying the monthly fee for that membership (e.g. Silver membership of £32 per month or Gold membership of £40 per month) to the period of membership used and subtracting from the price paid.

faqs.question: When will I receive my refund?
faqs.answer: Please be aware that refunds may take up to 6 - 8 weeks during peak times University admission periods, August - October and January. Online and in person card payments usually take up to 7 working days during non-peak periods and payment will be refunded back to the card that was used to make the initial payment.

faqs.question: When will my monthly payments end after submitting a cancellation request?
faqs.answer: Direct debit payments can be cancelled at any time by providing a full calendar month's notice in writing. At every opportunity we will try and cancel your membership as soon as possible. However, due to bank deadlines a payment may still be deducted after we receive your cancellation request. For example, if we receive your cancellation request on the 20 March, a membership payment will still be requested on the 1st or the next working day of April to pay for Aprils membership, meaning your membership won't expire until the 30 April.

faqs.question: Can I transfer my membership from one gym to the other?
faqs.answer: If you have a Silver membership you can upgrade to Gold which will allow access to Powerbase gym. Please email sportmemberships@lboro.ac.uk (mailto:sportmemberships@lboro.ac.uk) to request an upgrade. Gold membership includes access to both Holywell fitness centre and Powerbase gym.

faqs.question: Do I need to book to be able to swim in the pool?
faqs.answer: All sessions must be booked in advanced, either online, via the Loughborough Sport app or over the telephone (01509 226200). From September 1st, 2023 bookings for public swim sessions can be made up to two weeks in advance. Bookings for Performance Programme and AU Club sessions can be made up to 7 days in advance.

faqs.question: Can I access the building without a booking?
faqs.answer: Without a valid booking you won't be able to access through the turnstiles to the reception and onto the pool, even if you have a valid membership.

faqs.question: Are children allowed in public swim times?
faqs.answer: Children aged 12 years and above are welcome to book a lane swim but must be a competent swimmer and be able to swim continuous lengths.

faqs.question: Do I have to pay to use the pool as a member?
faqs.answer: No, if you have purchased either a Gold, Silver or Bronze membership, all levels allow you to access Loughborough University Swimming Pool during public swim sessions at no additional charge. Purple members will benefit by a 10% discount off the pay as you go price.

faqs.question: Do you have car parking information ?
faqs.answer: Our parking system is provided by APCOA and uses ANPR (Automatic Number Plate Recognition) technology to charge and enforce parking upon the University's campus. It is important that any vehicle coming onto campus is registered and parks in a designated bay. Students, staff or campus partners: Visitors • Must register on the Loughborough Universities parking portal • Student permits should be approved by the parking team before completing registration • Any Loughborough Sport member paying to use a sport facility through their Loughborough sport / swim school account is permitted up to 2 hours parking free of charge when inputting your car registration details on the tablets located near the reception desk. Tablets are located in the swimming pool, Holywell fitness Centre, Powerbase Gym, Tennis Centre, Hipac, and the Cricket centre. • Please ensure your car is registered with APCOA connect (https://www.apcoaconnect.com/) • Use your APCOA connect account to pay for any charges incurred beyond 2 hours. • If you fail to input your car registration into our facility tablets during every visit Loughborough Sport are not responsible for any charges or parking fines received.

faqs.question: How can I access the Athletics Centre?
faqs.answer: The Athletics Centre is available during Open sessions with a Gold membership or on a Pay-As-You-Go basis. For further information on access outside of the Open Public Sessions please see click the link below. • If you are a member of the community who is not eligible for a Gold Membership or Loughborough Sport Athletics Partner status, access to Open Public Sessions will be available on a PAYG basis at £5.50 a session with discounts available for Loughborough Sport Purple, Bronze and Silver Members. • If you have a current Gold Membership, either fixed term or pay by monthly direct debit, please note you will need to be in the AU Athletics Club or granted Loughborough Sport Athletics Partner status to access outside of Open Public Sessions.
